nodes.title: Validate stream input early
nodes.why: A clear guard gives a helpful error before any async work starts.
nodes.alternative: Assume input is valid and fail later when calling getReader.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more upfront checks, much better error clarity.

nodes.title: Create decoder with chosen encoding
nodes.why: This keeps decoding flexible while defaulting to utf-8 for common cases.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible API with almost no extra complexity.

nodes.title: Abort handler cancels the reader
nodes.why: Canceling the reader stops further reads and releases resources faster.
nodes.alternative: Only set an aborted flag and let read loop end naturally.
nodes.tradeoff: Direct cancel is cleaner and faster, but needs cancel support checks.

nodes.title: Handle already-aborted signal immediately
nodes.why: If cancellation already happened, we should stop before starting the read loop.
nodes.alternative: Always enter the loop and detect abort later.
nodes.tradeoff: Immediate exit avoids unnecessary work and race conditions.

nodes.title: Decode chunks in streaming mode
nodes.why: Streaming mode preserves multi-byte characters split across chunk boundaries.
nodes.alternative: Decode each chunk independently without stream mode.
nodes.tradeoff: Streaming decode is slightly more deliberate, but avoids corrupted text.

nodes.title: Flush decoder after loop ends
nodes.why: Final flush emits buffered trailing bytes that may not form a full char until the end.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra call, but correct output for edge-case byte boundaries.

nodes.title: Always remove abort listener
nodes.why: Cleanup avoids hanging listeners and subtle memory leaks.
nodes.alternative: Rely on GC and skip explicit removal.
nodes.tradeoff: A tiny cleanup step for safer long-lived apps.
